hypothesis: Incorporating explicit solvent molecule interactions through a hybrid implicit-explicit solvation scheme during ChEMBL3D dataset generation—rather than relying solely on CPCM implicit solvent—will reveal systematic conformational biases in current LoQI predictions for strongly amphiphilic drug-like molecules, and retraining LoQI on this augmented dataset will reduce the energy gap between predicted solution-phase minima and protein-bound crystal conformers in the Platinum Diverse benchmark by at least 30%, because explicit first-shell water molecules impose directional hydrogen-bonding constraints that reshape the accessible conformational landscape in ways that better approximate the polar microenvironment of protein binding pockets.
